sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Bologna and Juventus on 2025-05-04 at 18:45:00 promises to be an intriguing encounter at the Renato Dall'Ara stadium in Bologna. Bologna, currently sitting at 7th place with 61 points, will host Juventus, who are just one spot above in 6th with 62 points, in Round 35 of the league. With only a single point separating the two sides, this match could have significant implications for their final standings. The referee for the game, Daniele Doveri, averages 4.4 yellow cards and 0.21 red cards per game, hinting at a potentially feisty affair.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two teams, draws have been a common outcome with 4 out of 5 games ending level, while Juventus managed the sole victory. Their most recent encounter on 2024-12-07 at Juventus’ home ground resulted in a 2-2 draw, with Teun Koopmeiners earning the Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.8. Head-to-head streaks show Bologna have not lost to Juventus in their last 4 meetings, while Juventus have an impressive streak of 20 games without a loss against Bologna. Both teams have scored in 5 of their last 6 encounters, suggesting goals could be on the cards for both sides.

sections.subtitle: Bologna's Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Bologna will rely heavily on Riccardo Orsolini, a midfielder ranked 5th in the league for goals scored with 12, surpassing his expected goals (xG) of 8.25 by a significant margin of 3.75. Juan Miranda, a defender, also stands out with 54 key passes (5th in the league) and 10 big chances created (4th in the league), showcasing his attacking contributions from the back with an average rating of 7.17. Santiago Castro adds firepower up front with 8 goals and 4 assists. Bologna’s season stats are impressive, with 52 goals scored (7th in the league), an average ball possession of 58.47% (3rd), and 81 big chances created (5th). However, they’ve underperformed their xG by -6.67 (17th rank), indicating some inefficiency in front of goal.

sections.subtitle: Juventus' Key Performers and Form
sections.text: Juventus will look to Randal Kolo Muani, a forward with 6 goals, overperforming his xG of 3.46 by 2.54, demonstrating clinical finishing with an average rating of 7.09. Manuel Locatelli, a midfielder, has been in fine form over the last 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 7.58, contributing 1 goal recently. Khéphren Thuram also adds creativity in midfield with 5 assists and an average rating of 7.07. Juventus have shown strength in scoring early, with 22.58% of their goals coming between the 31st and 45th minutes, and they’ve been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 games against Bologna and in general.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Bologna enter this match with a streak of 3 games without a loss, while their home games against Juventus show no losses in the last 4 and a tendency to score first in 5 of the last 6 encounters. Juventus, on the other hand, have not lost in their last 20 games against Bologna, often taking the lead in the first half in 4 of their last 5 general matches. Both teams have struggled to keep clean sheets in head-to-heads, with Bologna failing to do so in 18 games against Juventus, and Juventus conceding in their last 4 against Bologna. Matches between these sides also tend to have fewer than 10.5 corners in 6 out of 6 recent head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Potential Impact
sections.text: Bologna will miss Dan Ndoye, a midfielder who leads the league in fouls drawn per game at 3.3, and has contributed 8 goals and 3 assists this season with a rating of 7.12. His absence could affect Bologna’s ability to win free kicks in dangerous areas. Juventus will be without Yıldız Kenan, a midfielder ranked 4th in the league for successful dribbles per game at 1.7, alongside key players like Teun Koopmeiners, potentially impacting their midfield creativity. These absences could tilt the balance slightly, depending on how both teams adapt.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Standouts and Game Expectations
sections.text: Bologna’s strength lies in their pressing game, ranking 4th in possessions won in the final third per game at 3.91, and penalty area entries per game at 28.68 (4th). They also excel in accurate crosses with 177 (3rd in the league). Juventus, meanwhile, have shown a knack for late goals, with 22.58% of their goals scored between the 76th and 90th minutes, which could be crucial if the game remains tight. Given Bologna’s 58.47% average possession (3rd) and Juventus’ ability to strike early, we could see a game of contrasting styles. Both teams’ tendency to score suggests a high likelihood of a competitive, goal-heavy match.
